text:
Lucas awoke with a start. There was a sensation of falling, of losing consciousness in the midst of a nightmare. His eyes darted around the unfamiliar bedroom. Everything was dark—no curtains, no windows. Anxiety coursed through his body as he realized it wasn’t just dark; it was silent.

He tried to sit up but felt dizziness brush over him. Taking a deep breath and pushing through, he pushed himself onto his knees. Crawling across the dirt floor, Lucas assessed his surroundings. There were creaks in the floorboards, and every so often, a distant sound, though he couldn’t distinguish its source.

Determined, he rose to his feet, wobbling slightly but steady now. He tried to recall what had set off his alarm. His phone was across the room, but he couldn’t reach it. His eyes darted around again, catching glints of light from a small, flickering light source close by.

He edged towards it and
